title_english: Heart Sūtra of the Perfection of Wisdom
source: GRETIL - Göttingen Register of Electronic Texts in Indian Languages
notes: Sanskrit text representing a back-translation from Chinese. Contains non-idiomatic vocabulary distinct from standard Prajñāpāramitā texts (e.g., kṣaya instead of nirodha for 'cessation').
text_iast:
Oṃ Namo Bhagavatyai Ārya-prajñāpāramitāyai!

Ārya-avalokiteśvaro bodhisattvo gambhīrāṃ prajñāpāramitācaryāṃ caramāṇo vyavalokayati sma: pañca-skandhās, tāṃś ca svabhāva-śūnyān paśyati sma.

Iha Śāriputra rūpaṃ śūnyatā śūnyataiva rūpam, rūpān na pṛthak śūnyatā śūnyatāyā na pṛthag rūpam, yad rūpaṃ sā śūnyatā yā śūnyatā tad rūpam; evam eva vedanā-saṃjñā-saṃskāra-vijñānāni.

Iha Śāriputra sarva-dharmāḥ śūnyatā-lakṣaṇā, anutpannā aniruddhā, amalā avimalā, anūnā aparipūrṇāḥ.

Tasmāc Chāriputra śūnyatāyāṃ na rūpaṃ na vedanā na saṃjñā na saṃskārāḥ na vijñānam. Na cakṣuḥ-śrotra-ghrāṇa-jihvā-kāya-manāṃsi. Na rūpa-śabda-gandha-rasa-spraṣṭavya-dharmāḥ. Na cakṣur-dhātur yāvan na manovijñāna-dhātuḥ. Na-avidyā na-avidyā-kṣayo yāvan na jarā-maraṇaṃ na jarā-maraṇa-kṣayo. Na duḥkha-samudaya-nirodha-mārgā. Na jñānaṃ, na prāptir na-aprāptiḥ.

Tasmāc Chāriputra aprāptitvād bodhisattvasya prajñāpāramitām āśritya viharaty acittāvaraṇaḥ. Cittāvaraṇa-nāstitvād atrastro viparyāsa-atikrānto niṣṭhā-nirvāṇa-prāptaḥ.

Tryadhva-vyavasthitāḥ sarva-buddhāḥ prajñāpāramitām āśrityānuttarāṃ samyak-saṃbodhim abhisaṃbuddhāḥ.

Tasmāj jñātavyam: prajñāpāramitā mahā-mantro mahā-vidyā-mantro 'nuttara-mantro 'samasama-mantraḥ, sarva-duḥkha-praśamanaḥ, satyam amithyatvāt.

Prajñāpāramitāyām ukto mantraḥ. Tadyathā: Oṃ gate gate pāragate pārasaṃgate bodhi svāhā.

Ity ārya-prajñāpāramitā-hṛdayaṃ samāptam.
text_devanagari:
ॐ नमो भगवत्यै आर्य-प्रज्ञापारमितायै!

आर्य-अवलोकितेश्वरो बोधिसत्त्वो गम्भीरां प्रज्ञापारमिताचर्यां चरमाणो व्यवलोकयति स्म: पञ्च-स्कन्धास्, तांश्च स्वभाव-शून्यान् पश्यति स्म।

इह शारिपुत्र रूपं शून्यता शून्यतैव रूपम्, रूपान् न पृथक् शून्यता शून्यतायाः न पृथग् रूपम्, यद् रूपं सा शून्यता या शून्यता तद् रूपम्; एवमेव वेदना-संज्ञा-संस्कार-विज्ञानानि।

इह शारिपुत्र सर्व-धर्माः शून्यता-लक्षणा, अनुत्पन्ना अनिरुद्धा, अमला अविमला, अनूना अपरिपूर्णाः।

तस्माच् छारिपुत्र शून्यतायां न रूपं न वेदना न संज्ञा न संस्काराः न विज्ञानम्। न चक्षुः-श्रोत्र-घ्राण-जिह्वा-काय-मनांसि। न रूप-शब्द-गन्ध-रस-स्प्रष्टव्य-धर्माः। न चक्षुर्-धातुर् यावन् न मनोविज्ञान-धातुः। न-अविद्या न-अविद्या-क्षयो यावन् न जरा-मरणं न जरा-मरण-क्षयो। न दुःख-समुदय-निरोध-मार्गा। न ज्ञानं, न प्राप्तिर् न-अप्राप्तिः।

तस्माच् छारिपुत्र अप्राप्तित्वाद् बोधिसत्त्वस्य प्रज्ञापारमितामाश्रित्य विहरत्यचित्तावरणः। चित्तावरण-नास्तित्वाद् अत्रस्त्रो विपर्यास-अतिक्रान्तो निष्ठा-निर्वाण-प्राप्तः।

त्र्यध्व-व्यवस्थिताः सर्व-बुद्धाः प्रज्ञापारमितामाश्रित्यानुत्तरां सम्यक्-संबोधिमभिसंबुद्धाः।

तस्माज् ज्ञातव्यम्: प्रज्ञापारमिता महा-मन्त्रो महा-विद्या-मन्त्रो ऽनुत्तर-मन्त्रो ऽसमसम-मन्त्रः, सर्व-दुःख-प्रशमनः, सत्यम् अमिथ्यत्वात्।

प्रज्ञापारमितायामुक्तो मन्त्रः। तद्यथा: ॐ गते गते पारगते पारसंगते बोधि स्वाहा।

इत्यार्य-प्रज्ञापारमिता-हृदयं समाप्तम्।

segments.english_gloss: Oṃ! Homage to the Noble Lady Prajñāpāramitā!
segments.note: Maṅgala absent from Chinese T251 and most Sanskrit short-text witnesses. Late Tantric interpolation from Nepalese manuscripts (Attwood 2024). If salutation needed, namas sarvajñāya is more historically appropriate.

segments.iast: Ārya-avalokiteśvaro bodhisattvo gambhīrāṃ prajñāpāramitācaryāṃ caramāṇo vyavalokayati sma: pañca-skandhās, tāṃś ca svabhāva-śūnyān paśyati sma.
segments.devanagari: आर्य-अवलोकितेश्वरो बोधिसत्त्वो गम्भीरां प्रज्ञापारमिताचर्यां चरमाणो व्यवलोकयति स्म: पञ्च-स्कन्धास्, तांश्च स्वभाव-शून्यान् पश्यति स्म।
segments.english_gloss: The noble Avalokiteśvara Bodhisattva, while practicing the profound prajñāpāramitā course, looked down and saw the five aggregates, and he saw them as empty of intrinsic nature.
segments.note: Section I error (Attwood 2015): pañca-skandhās should be pañcaskandhāṃs (accusative). The skandhas are objects of vyavalokayati sma ('examined'), not nominative subjects. Also: vyavalokayati sma uses imperfect tense unusual for sūtra style.

segments.iast: Iha Śāriputra rūpaṃ śūnyatā śūnyataiva rūpam, rūpān na pṛthak śūnyatā śūnyatāyā na pṛthag rūpam, yad rūpaṃ sā śūnyatā yā śūnyatā tad rūpam; evam eva vedanā-saṃjñā-saṃskāra-vijñānāni.
segments.devanagari: इह शारिपुत्र रूपं शून्यता शून्यतैव रूपम्, रूपान् न पृथक् शून्यता शून्यतायाः न पृथग् रूपम्, यद् रूपं सा शून्यता या शून्यता तद् रूपम्; एवमेव वेदना-संज्ञा-संस्कार-विज्ञानानि।
segments.english_gloss: Here, Śāriputra, form is emptiness, and emptiness itself is form. Emptiness is not separate from form; form is not separate from emptiness. That which is form is emptiness; that which is emptiness is form. Just so are feeling, perception, formation, and consciousness.
segments.note: The phrase 'yad rūpaṃ sā śūnyatā yā śūnyatā tad rūpam' has no Chinese counterpart and is absent from most Conze witnesses. Late interpolation not in Large Sutra parallels (Nattier 1992, Attwood 2017a). Original Aṣṭa reading was 'rūpam eva māyā māyaiva rūpam' (appearance is only illusion).

segments.iast: Iha Śāriputra sarva-dharmāḥ śūnyatā-lakṣaṇā, anutpannā aniruddhā, amalā avimalā, anūnā aparipūrṇāḥ.
segments.devanagari: इह शारिपुत्र सर्व-धर्माः शून्यता-लक्षणा, अनुत्पन्ना अनिरुद्धा, अमला अविमला, अनूना अपरिपूर्णाः।
segments.english_gloss: Here, Śāriputra, all dharmas are marked by emptiness: not arisen, not ceased, not defiled, not undefiled, not deficient, not complete.

segments.iast: Tasmāc Chāriputra śūnyatāyāṃ na rūpaṃ na vedanā na saṃjñā na saṃskārāḥ na vijñānam.
segments.devanagari: तस्माच् छारिपुत्र शून्यतायां न रूपं न वेदना न संज्ञा न संस्काराः न विज्ञानम्।
segments.english_gloss: Therefore, Śāriputra, in emptiness there is no form, no feeling, no perception, no formations, no consciousness.
No eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, or mind. No form, sound, smell, taste, tangible, or mental object.
No eye-element, up to and including no mind-consciousness element.

segments.iast: Na-avidyā na-avidyā-kṣayo yāvan na jarā-maraṇaṃ na jarā-maraṇa-kṣayo.
segments.devanagari: न-अविद्या न-अविद्या-क्षयो यावन् न जरा-मरणं न जरा-मरण-क्षयो।
segments.english_gloss: No ignorance, no destruction of ignorance, up to and including no old age and death, no destruction of old age and death.
segments.note: Uses kṣaya (destruction) instead of standard nirodha (cessation) - evidence of back-translation (Nattier 1992)

segments.iast: Na duḥkha-samudaya-nirodha-mārgā. Na jñānaṃ, na prāptir na-aprāptiḥ.
segments.devanagari: न दुःख-समुदय-निरोध-मार्गा। न ज्ञानं, न प्राप्तिर् न-अप्राप्तिः।
segments.english_gloss: No suffering, origination, cessation, or path. No knowledge, no attainment, and no non-attainment.
segments.note: na-aprāptiḥ is a late interpolation not found in any Large Sutra version (Huifeng 2014). Should be 'na prāptir nābhisamayaḥ' (no attainment, no realization). The pair prāpti/abhisamaya refers to mārga/phala (path and fruition). Kumārajīva's T223 error was copied into the Heart Sutra (Attwood 2020a).

segments.iast: Tasmāc Chāriputra aprāptitvād bodhisattvasya prajñāpāramitām āśritya viharaty acittāvaraṇaḥ. Cittāvaraṇa-nāstitvād atrastro viparyāsa-atikrānto niṣṭhā-nirvāṇa-prāptaḥ.
segments.devanagari: तस्माच् छारिपुत्र अप्राप्तित्वाद् बोधिसत्त्वस्य प्रज्ञापारमितामाश्रित्य विहरत्यचित्तावरणः। चित्तावरण-नास्तित्वाद् अत्रस्त्रो विपर्यास-अतिक्रान्तो निष्ठा-निर्वाण-प्राप्तः।
segments.english_gloss: Therefore, Śāriputra, because of non-attainment, the bodhisattva, relying on prajñāpāramitā, dwells with mind unobstructed. Because of the absence of mental obstruction, he is unafraid, has gone beyond error, and has attained ultimate nirvāṇa.
segments.note: Section VI error (Attwood 2018a, 2020a): Conze's wrongly placed full stop creates a sentence fragment. niṣṭhānirvāṇaḥ is a bahuvrīhi compound (masculine adjective) meaning 'one whose nirvāṇa is complete' - it describes the bodhisattva, not a separate neuter noun. Corrected reading removes the period: 'viharaty acittāvaraṇaś cittāvaraṇanāstitvād atrasto viparyāsātikrānto niṣṭhānirvāṇaḥ'.

segments.iast: Tryadhva-vyavasthitāḥ sarva-buddhāḥ prajñāpāramitām āśrityānuttarāṃ samyak-saṃbodhim abhisaṃbuddhāḥ.
segments.english_gloss: All buddhas abiding in the three times, relying on prajñāpāramitā, have fully awakened to unsurpassed, perfect, complete enlightenment.

segments.iast: Tasmāj jñātavyam: prajñāpāramitā mahā-mantro mahā-vidyā-mantro 'nuttara-mantro 'samasama-mantraḥ, sarva-duḥkha-praśamanaḥ, satyam amithyatvāt.
segments.devanagari: तस्माज् ज्ञातव्यम्: प्रज्ञापारमिता महा-मन्त्रो महा-विद्या-मन्त्रो ऽनुत्तर-मन्त्रो ऽसमसम-मन्त्रः, सर्व-दुःख-प्रशमनः, सत्यम् अमिथ्यत्वात्।
segments.english_gloss: Therefore it should be known: prajñāpāramitā is the great mantra, the mantra of great knowledge, the unsurpassed mantra, the mantra equal to the unequalled, which allays all suffering — true, because not false.
segments.note: Epithets section (Attwood 2017b): Standard Prajñāpāramitā has three epithets (mahā-, anuttara-, asamasama-) representing vidyā, not mantra. Gilgit Pañc has 'mahāvidyeyaṃ...anuttareyaṃ vidyā...asamasameyaṃ vidyā'. Chinese míngzhòu 明咒 represents vidyā. The fourth epithet shénzhòu 神咒 may be a synonym for míngzhòu.

segments.iast: Prajñāpāramitāyām ukto mantraḥ. Tadyathā: Oṃ gate gate pāragate pārasaṃgate bodhi svāhā.
segments.devanagari: प्रज्ञापारमितायामुक्तो मन्त्रः। तद्यथा: ॐ गते गते पारगते पारसंगते बोधि स्वाहा।
segments.english_gloss: The mantra is proclaimed in the prajñāpāramitā. It runs thus: Oṃ gate gate pāragate pārasaṃgate bodhi svāhā.
segments.note: Oṃ before 'gate gate' is absent from most Sanskrit manuscripts including the Hōryūji manuscript, and from all Chinese versions except T.257 (11th century Dānapāla translation). Late tantric interpolation (Attwood 2024). Conze (1975) omits oṃ from his translation.

segments.english_gloss: Thus concludes the Noble Heart of the Perfection of Wisdom.
segments.note: Colophon not present in Chinese T251

scholarly_refs: Conze, Edward (1967). 'The Prajñāpāramitā-Hṛdaya Sūtra' in Thirty Years of Buddhist Studies, pp. 147-167.
Nattier, Jan (1992). 'The Heart Sūtra: a Chinese apocryphal text?' JIABS 15(2): 153-223.
Attwood, Jayarava (2015). 'Heart Murmurs: Some Problems with Conze's Prajñāpāramitāhṛdaya.' JOCBS 8: 28-48.
Attwood, Jayarava (2017a). 'Form is (Not) Emptiness: The Enigma at the Heart of the Heart Sutra.' JOCBS 13: 52-80.
Attwood, Jayarava (2017b). 'Epithets of the Mantra in the Heart Sutra.' JOCBS 12: 26-57.
Attwood, Jayarava (2018a). 'A Note on Niṣṭhānirvāṇaḥ in the Heart Sutra.' JOCBS 14: 10-17.
Attwood, Jayarava (2020a). 'Ungarbling Section VI of the Heart Sutra.' JOCBS 18: 11-41.
Attwood, Jayarava (2020c). 'The History of the Heart Sutra as a Palimpsest.' Pacific World Series 4, no.1: 155-182.
Attwood, Jayarava (2023). 'Heart to Heart: A Comparative Study of and Commentary on the Chinese and Sanskrit Heart Sutra Texts.' Buddhist Studies Review 40(2): 159-188.
Attwood, Jayarava (2024). 'Revised Editions of the Prajñāpāramitāhṛdaya and Bānrěbōluómìduō xīn jīng.' Asian Literature and Translation 11(1): 1-42.
Huifeng (2014). 'Apocryphal Treatment for Conze's Heart Problems: Non-attainment, Apprehension, and Mental Hanging in the Prajñāpāramitā Hṛdaya.' JOCBS 6: 72-105.
Kotyk, Jeffrey (2019). 'Chinese State and Buddhist Historical Sources on Xuanzang.' T'oung Pao 105(5-6): 513-544.
Karashima, Seishi et al. (2016). Mahāyāna Texts: Prajñāpāramitā Texts (1). Gilgit Manuscripts in the National Archives of India.

section_I_error.description: Conze's pañca-skandhās (nominative plural) should be pañcaskandhāṃs (accusative plural). The skandhas are objects of vyavalokayati sma ('examined'), not a separate nominative subject.
section_I_error.corrected_reading: pañcaskandhāṃs tāṃś ca svabhāvaśūnyān paśyati sma

section_VI_error.description: Conze's wrongly placed full stop between acittāvaraṇaḥ and cittāvaraṇanāstitvād creates a sentence fragment. niṣṭhānirvāṇaḥ is a bahuvrīhi compound (masculine nominative singular adjective) meaning 'one whose nirvāṇa is complete', describing the bodhisattva.
section_VI_error.corrected_reading: viharaty acittāvaraṇaś cittāvaraṇanāstitvād atrasto viparyāsātikrānto niṣṭhānirvāṇaḥ

na_aprapti_interpolation.description: na-aprāptiḥ does not occur in any version of the Large Sutra. Should be na prāptir nābhisamayaḥ ('no attainment, no realization'). The pair refers to mārga/phala (path and fruition).

mangala_interpolation.description: The maṅgala (Oṃ namo Bhagavatyai Ārya-Prajñāpāramitāyai) is absent from Chinese versions and most Sanskrit sources of the short text. It reflects late Tantric influence from Nepalese manuscripts.

yad_rupam_interpolation.description: yad rūpaṃ sā śūnyatā yā śūnyatā tad rūpaṃ has no counterpart in Chinese texts and is absent from most of Conze's witnesses. Late interpolation not found in Large Sutra parallels.

om_in_mantra.description: Oṃ before gate gate is absent from most Sanskrit manuscripts including the Hōryūji manuscript, and from all Chinese versions except T.257 (11th century). Late Tantric interpolation.
